questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Rapaciousness
questions.explanation: Explanation: Rapaciousness refers to extreme greed or an excessive desire for wealth or possessions. Generosity denotes a willingness to give freely and share with others, often at a personal cost. The meanings of other options are: Collaboration involves working jointly with others, which is not directly related to the concept of greed or generosity. Uncertain describes a state of being unsure or not confident, unrelated to the context of greed or generosity. Occupation refers to a person's job or profession, which doesn't pertain to the traits of rapaciousness or generosity.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. When a beggar appeared, / I offered him / an one rupee coin / but he did not take it.
options: but he did not take it
questions.explanation: Explanation: Article Usage: The indefinite article "an" is used before words that begin with a vowel sound, while "a" is used before words that begin with a consonant sound. Pronunciation of "One": The word "one" begins with a consonant sound (, despite starting with the vowel letter 'o'. Therefore, it should be preceded by "a", not "an". Corrected Segment: "A one rupee coin"

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Keep body and soul together
options: A sudden change in fortune due to a windfall
To earn a sufficient amount of money in order to keep yourself alive.
To be young and without experience
questions.explanation: Explanation: The idiom "keep body and soul together" means "to earn a sufficient amount of money in order to keep yourself alive." This idiom refers to managing to survive by earning just enough to provide for basic necessities like food, clothing, and shelter.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. Although Tom's only an amateur, he's a first-class player.
questions.explanation: Explanation: Amateur: A person who engages in an activity, especially a sport or hobby, without payment; non-professional. Professional: A person engaged or qualified in a profession, especially one that involves prolonged training and a formal qualification; opposite of amateur. The meanings of other options are: Procrastination: The action of delaying or postponing something; not related to the concept of amateurism. Paragon: A person or thing regarded as a perfect example of a particular quality; not an antonym of amateur. Adjourn: To suspend proceedings to a later time or date; unrelated to the term amateur.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error. A Himalayas stretch across / the north-east part / of India and pass / through many countries.
questions.explanation: Explanation: Article Usage: The correct phrase should be "The Himalayas" instead of "A Himalayas". The definite article "The" is used with mountain ranges, such as "The Himalayas". Subject-Verb Agreement: The subject "The Himalayas" is plural; therefore, the verb should be "stretch" to agree in number. The corrected sentence is: "The Himalayas stretch across the north-east part of India and pass through many countries."

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined words. Ashamed and shy
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate one-word substitute for "ashamed and shy" is "embarrassed". Embarrassed: Feeling or showing embarrassment, which encompasses feelings of shame and shyness. The other options are: Squeamish: Easily nauseated or disgusted; not related to feeling ashamed or shy. Impregnable: Unable to be captured or broken into; unrelated to emotions of shame or shyness. Harangue: A lengthy and aggressive speech; does not pertain to feelings of shame or shyness.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. My friend said to me, “Do you know the recipe of mango ice-cream?”
options: My friend asked me if I know the recipe of mango ice-cream.
My friend asked me if I knew the recipe of mango ice-cream.
My friend asked me that I knew the recipe of mango ice-cream.
My friend asked me that I know the recipe of mango ice-cream.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • Reporting Verb Change: "Said to" changes to "asked" when converting a direct question into indirect speech. • Conjunction: The conjunction "if" is used to introduce the reported yes/no question. • Pronoun Adjustment: The pronoun "you" (referring to the person being spoken to) remains "me" in indirect speech. • Tense Shift: The present tense "know" changes to the past tense "knew" to reflect the back-shifting of tenses in reported speech. Therefore, the correct option is: "My friend asked me if I knew the recipe of mango ice-cream."

questions.question: Select the sentence that has a spelling error.
options: The spread of various diseases is intricately linked to the deteriorating state of public health conditions.
Our health depends not just on our nutrition and personal hygeine but also on how we clean our cities and surroundings.
Keeping cities clean and free of germs is particularly essential for keeping their residents healthy.
The good news is that waste management, to keep cities clean, is now getting attention through the Swachh Bharat Mission.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The word "hygeine" is misspelled. The correct spelling is "hygiene", which refers to conditions or practices conducive to maintaining health and preventing disease, especially through cleanliness.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The Warrens are __________ their house by the end of this month.
questions.explanation: Explanation: "Going to shift": This phrase indicates a planned action in the near future. The use of "going to" conveys that the Warrens have already decided and arranged to move their house by the end of this month.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. He was the most cleverest man I ever knew.
questions.explanation: Explanation: Superlative Form: The term "cleverest" is the superlative form of the adjective "clever," indicating the highest degree of cleverness among a group. Corrected Sentence: Therefore, the sentence should be: "He was the cleverest man I ever knew."

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Are they watching the programmes?
options: Were the programmes being watched by them?
Are the programmes being watched by them?
Had the programmes been watched by them?
Have the programmes been watched by them?
questions.explanation: Explanation: • Object as New Subject: "the programmes" becomes the subject. • Appropriate Form of 'Be': Since the original sentence is in the present continuous tense ("are watching"), the passive form requires "are being." • Past Participle of the Main Verb: The past participle of "watch" is "watched." • Agent Introduction: Introduce the original subject with "by them." Constructing the Sentence: • Combine these elements to form: "Are the programmes being watched by them?"

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. Calling me a friend, Surjit Singh encouraged me and advised me not to lose heart.
options: Surjit Singh demanded, “Cheer up, friend. Do not lose heart.”
Surjit Singh asked me, “Cheer up, friend. Do not lose heart.”
Surjit Singh ordered me, “Cheer up, friend. Do not lose heart.”
Surjit Singh said, “Cheer up, friend. Do not lose heart.”
questions.explanation: Explanation: Direct Speech: This form quotes the exact words spoken by the speaker, enclosed in quotation marks. Contextual Clues: The original sentence indicates that Surjit Singh referred to the listener as "friend," provided encouragement, and advised not to lose heart. Appropriate Reporting Verb: The verb "said" is neutral and appropriately introduces direct speech without implying force or command. Imperative Sentences: The phrases "Cheer up" and "Do not lose heart" are imperative sentences used to give advice or encouragement. The sentence "Calling me a friend, Surjit Singh encouraged me and advised me not to lose heart." can be expressed in direct speech as: "Surjit Singh said, 'Cheer up, friend. Do not lose heart.'"

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. When is this car driven by you?
options: When do you drive this car?
When should you drive this car?
When will you drive this car?
When did you drive this car?
questions.explanation: Explanation: Tense and Auxiliary Verb: • The original sentence is in the present simple tense, indicated by "is driven." • In questions using the present simple tense, the auxiliary verb "do" is used. Formulating the Question: • Place the auxiliary verb "do" before the subject "you." • Follow with the base form of the main verb "drive." • Add the object "this car." • Precede the question with "When" to inquire about the time. Therefore, the active voice question is: "When do you drive this car?"

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. They think there are some bitter learning experiences B. Which, over a period of time, guide them to take a good path C. Many successful people believe that D. There is no failure in anybody’s life
questions.explanation: Explanation: C: "Many successful people believe that" • This introductory sentence sets the context by mentioning the beliefs of successful individuals. D: "There is no failure in anybody’s life" • This statement presents the belief held by successful people, directly following the introduction. A: "They think there are some bitter learning experiences" • This sentence elaborates on the previous one, explaining that what might be perceived as failures are actually challenging learning experiences. B: "Which, over a period of time, guide them to take a good path" • This concluding sentence explains the outcome of these learning experiences, emphasizing their positive impact over time. The correct sequence to form a coherent paragraph is: CDAB.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined words. The traffic sergeant proved through the test that Raj was under the influence of drugs and alcohol .
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate one-word substitute for "under the influence of drugs and alcohol" is "inebriated." Inebriated: This term means intoxicated or drunk, indicating that someone is under the influence of alcohol or drugs. The other options are: Simonised: This refers to polishing a vehicle, derived from the brand name "Simoniz." Ponied: This means to pay a sum of money, especially as a contribution or unavoidable expense. Pocked: This describes something marked or indented with small hollows or pits.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Get your act together
questions.explanation: Explanation: The idiom "get your act together" means to get organized and work efficiently. Meaning: This phrase is used to advise someone to start organizing themselves so that they can perform tasks effectively. Usage: It's often employed when someone is being disorganized or ineffective, suggesting they need to improve their performance.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Hind
questions.explanation: Explanation: The word "hind" refers to the rear or back part of something, especially in animals. Therefore, the most appropriate synonym from the given options is "rear". Rear: Refers to the back part of something, aligning with the meaning of "hind". The meanings of other options are: Instigate: Means to initiate or provoke an action or event. This is unrelated to the meaning of "hind". Fervour: Denotes intense and passionate feeling. This does not relate to "hind". Demarcation: Means the action of fixing boundaries or limits. This is unrelated to "hind".

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Peace of mind is commonly correlated with happiness, contentment, tranquility, contemplation, exercise, and relaxation. B. Thus, we all need peace of mind in our lives. C. Peace of mind, thus, gives us all these attributes. D. One of the most important aspects of the mind is getting peace.
questions.explanation: Explanation: D: "One of the most important aspects of the mind is getting peace." • This introductory sentence establishes the significance of attaining peace of mind. A: "Peace of mind is commonly correlated with happiness, contentment, tranquility, contemplation, exercise, and relaxation." • This sentence elaborates on what peace of mind is associated with, following the introduction. C: "Peace of mind, thus, gives us all these attributes." • This sentence connects the previous one by indicating that achieving peace of mind results in the mentioned attributes. B: "Thus, we all need peace of mind in our lives." • This concluding sentence emphasizes the necessity of peace of mind for everyone, summarizing the paragraph. The correct sequence to form a coherent paragraph is: D, A, C, B.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The tiger looked at me with a serious ________.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate word to fill in the blank is "stare". Stare: A fixed, intense look, often conveying seriousness or aggression. A tiger looking with a serious stare suggests a focused and possibly threatening gaze. The other options are: Smile: An expression of pleasure or friendliness, typically not associated with tigers, especially in serious contexts. Wink: A quick closing and opening of one eye, usually as a signal or in jest; not a behavior attributed to tigers. Nap: A short sleep; unrelated to the act of looking.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Impress
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate synonym for "impress" from the given options is "impact". Impact: Refers to a strong effect or influence, aligning with one of the meanings of "impress". The Meanings of other options are: Escalate: Means to increase or intensify; not synonymous with "impress". Scrutinize: Means to examine closely; unrelated to "impress". Growl: A low guttural sound made by an animal; not related to "impress".

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Do you know that you (1)______ about one-third of your life sleeping? However, your body doesn’t (2)______ the sleeping time. It has a lot to do (3)______ you are sleeping. The body produces energy at night (4)______ you have plenty of energy the next morning. Hormones, which are (5)______ growth chemicals, help the body to repair itself and grow. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The most appropriate word to fill in the blank is "spend". The Meanings of options are: Spend: Means to pass or use time in a particular manner. In this context, it indicates the amount of time dedicated to sleeping. Apply: Means to put something into operation or use; not suitable in this context. Delay: Means to postpone or put off; does not fit the context of the sentence. Settle: Means to resolve or establish; not relevant to the context of spending time sleeping.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Do you know that you (1)______ about one-third of your life sleeping? However, your body doesn’t (2)______ the sleeping time. It has a lot to do (3)______ you are sleeping. The body produces energy at night (4)______ you have plenty of energy the next morning. Hormones, which are (5)______ growth chemicals, help the body to repair itself and grow. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The sentence suggests that, despite sleeping, the body remains active and does not squander this time. The word "waste" accurately conveys the idea of not using time inefficiently. The most appropriate word to fill in the blank is "waste".

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Do you know that you (1)______ about one-third of your life sleeping? However, your body doesn’t (2)______ the sleeping time. It has a lot to do (3)______ you are sleeping. The body produces energy at night (4)______ you have plenty of energy the next morning. Hormones, which are (5)______ growth chemicals, help the body to repair itself and grow. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The phrase "It has a lot to do" suggests that the following word should connect to a specific time or condition in which something important is happening. The context indicates that the body's activities, such as producing energy and repairing itself, occur during the time you are sleeping. "When" is the appropriate word to indicate the time these actions take place.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Do you know that you (1)______ about one-third of your life sleeping? However, your body doesn’t (2)______ the sleeping time. It has a lot to do (3)______ you are sleeping. The body produces energy at night (4)______ you have plenty of energy the next morning. Hormones, which are (5)______ growth chemicals, help the body to repair itself and grow. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4.
questions.explanation: Explanation: "So that" indicates purpose or intent, which fits here because the body produces energy at night with the specific purpose of ensuring you have energy the next morning. The sentence implies a cause-and-effect relationship: the body produces energy at night for the purpose of replenishing energy levels by the morning.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Do you know that you (1)______ about one-third of your life sleeping? However, your body doesn’t (2)______ the sleeping time. It has a lot to do (3)______ you are sleeping. The body produces energy at night (4)______ you have plenty of energy the next morning. Hormones, which are (5)______ growth chemicals, help the body to repair itself and grow. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5.
questions.explanation: Explanation: "Special" is appropriate because it highlights the unique and specific role that hormones play in aiding the body’s growth and repair processes. Hormones are indeed vital and have distinct functions in the body, making "special" the most fitting description.
